content: The 2024-2025 SOTA trend of compressing and contextually routing structured long-term memory in active learning agents can be repurposed to solve a core open problem: how general agents maintain grounded, non-forgetting curiosity without catastrophic drift of intrinsic reward signals. Instead of recalculating novelty scores for every visited state from scratch, an active learning agent’s memory prioritization framework can dynamically retain only the high-uncertainty states that drive meaningful exploration, pruning redundant curious observations that waste compute.
